summary_one_line: Input must be a parseable sfnt font with valid table tags so shaping resolves a script reaching the RTL case block

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed TrueType/OpenType sfnt header (magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0x00) followed by recognized 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'MATH'/'GPOS' at offset 0x0c) so the font parses far enough that hb_script_get_horizontal_direction is reached with a script that falls into the contiguous RTL case block (line 589-654) containing case HB_SCRIPT_OLD_TURKIC at line 615.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winning seeds start with the sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 00 01 and carry valid ASCII FOURCC table tags ('MATH' at 0x0c in seed 1, 'GPOS' in seeds 2-5), forming a structurally valid font that drives the shaper down to the blocker case arm; the branch is taken 14 times in the winner role (T=14) vs 0 in the loser role.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds begin with non-magic bytes (ef/ff/99/e1 at offset 0x00) and lack the table-tag region, so the font fails to parse into a shapeable buffer; they never reach the OLD_TURKIC case (L1 615: T=0 F=191) and instead spend execution in fallback/mark-positioning code paths.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where harfbuzz's sfnt-header and table-tag comparisons recorded the expected literals (magic word and FOURCC tags), then substitutes those literals into the input at the matching offsets, synthesizing the valid header/tags that naive must stumble onto by blind havoc mutation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case HB_SCRIPT_OLD_TURKIC arm inside the switch on the script tag, whose true side resolves to HB_DIRECTION_RTL.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: L587 switch, L615 blocker, L654 return

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds begin with the sfnt magic word and carry ASCII FOURCC table tags such as MATH and GPOS.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: W seed 1 offset 0x00-0x0f, W seeds 2-5 offset 0x0c

evidence_trail.claim: Multiple winning seeds carry the GPOS table tag at the tag region.
The byte diff shows the winner concentrates the FOURCC tag bytes (G/P/O/S, M/A/T/H) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f where the loser has no such structure.
Loser-blocking seeds start with non-magic bytes rather than the sfnt header.
The loser never takes the OLD_TURKIC case while the winner takes it 14 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=14 F=147  T=0 F=191  case HB_SCRIPT_OLD_TURKIC:

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability and must reach the magic/tag literals by blind havoc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5334 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed ae1635cd677d2b15`; the ancestor chain has 24 levels with two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 22 and 23, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog build (dash rows are cmplog/vpc-exclusive vs naive).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the script tag is set directly without requiring a parseable font header/table-tag CMP chain shows no cmplog>naive divergence at the OLD_TURKIC arm, indicating the I2S advantage was not from solving the header/tag literal comparisons.

weakest_evidence_point: It is inferred but not directly observed that the RTL script selected at runtime is OLD_TURKIC specifically rather than another script in the same contiguous case block sharing the same return HB_DIRECTION_RTL.
